content.text:
Design a Workforce Learning & Procurement Reconciliation Portal to let organizations manage employee learning paths alongside vendor order governance and settlement in a fully offline environment. Users include Learners, Procurement Specialists, Approvers, Finance Analysts, Content Moderators, and System Administrators. The React web UI provides local username/password sign-in, optional MFA via time-based one-time passwords generated in an authenticator app, and role-based menus that only reveal permitted pages and actions. Learners can search a unified library of internal resources and job/skill tags using fast on-site full-text search with typo tolerance, optional synonym and pinyin matching, and multi-dimensional filters for date, category, and tags; results can be sorted by relevance, popularity, or most recent, with clear “why recommended” cues. The experience supports intelligent recommendations driven by viewing/completion behavior and tags, including cold-start defaults by job family, deduplication across near-duplicate resources, and diversity controls that ensure no more than 40% of a recommendation carousel comes from a single category. Learners enroll in learning paths with required/elective rules (for example, 6 required items plus any 2 electives) and track progress down to each resource, with resume learning, cross-device sync within the same offline network, and export of learning records to a downloadable CSV. Procurement and Finance users review orders and disputes through a guided workflow: ratings with text and image attachments, merchant replies, negative-review appeals with evidence uploads, and arbitration outcomes that control whether a review is hidden, shown with a disclaimer, or restored.

The backend uses Go with Echo to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the decoupled React frontend, and PostgreSQL persists all business data locally, including users, roles/permissions, learning paths, resource progress events, job/skill taxonomy, reviews, disputes, billing rules, and settlement ledgers. Search indexing is built and maintained on-premise: tokenization, weighting, and fuzzy match are executed locally, with nightly rebuilds and incremental updates on content changes; archive pages are auto-generated by month and tag based on persisted publish dates. The job/skill taxonomy supports hierarchical relationships, synonym consolidation, and conflict detection rules (for example, blocking two active synonyms pointing to different canonical tags), with an admin review queue and audit trails. Reconciliation supports configurable warehouse and transportation billing rules, generation of AR/AP entries, statement comparison, variance write-off thresholds (for example, variances under $5.00 auto-suggest write-off but still require Finance approval), settlement status transitions, and cost allocation by department and cost center, with exports compatible with downstream finance systems via offline file drop or LAN-based webhooks. Security includes salted password hashing, encryption at rest for sensitive fields (such as recovery secrets and dispute evidence metadata), masking of personal data in non-admin views, configurable session timeouts (15 minutes idle, 8 hours max), and a centralized configuration center with feature flags, parameters, phased rollout by user role, scheduled jobs with retry/compensation, and app version compatibility checks that block unsupported clients while allowing read-only access for up to 14 days.

1	-- Workforce Learning & Procurement Reconciliation Portal
2	-- Database Initialization Script
3	-- PostgreSQL 15+

6	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;

15	
16	-- ============================================================
17	-- ROLES & PERMISSIONS
18	-- ============================================================
19	
20	CREATE TABLE roles (

26	);
27	
28	CREATE TABLE permissions (
29	    id          SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,

32	    module      VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
33	    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
34	);
35	
36	CREATE TABLE role_permissions (
37	    role_id       INT NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
38	    permission_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES permissions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
39	    PRIMARY KEY (role_id, permission_id)
40	);
41	
42	-- ============================================================
43	-- USERS & AUTH
44	-- ============================================================
45	
46	CREATE TABLE users (
47	    id                  SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
48	    username            VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
49	    email               VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
50	    password_hash       TEXT NOT NULL,
51	    display_name        VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
52	    mfa_enabled         BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
53	    mfa_secret_enc      BYTEA,                          -- AES-256 encrypted TOTP secret
54	    mfa_recovery_enc    BYTEA,                          -- AES-256 encrypted recovery codes
55	    job_family          VARCHAR(100),
56	    department          VARCHAR(100),
57	    cost_center         VARCHAR(50),
58	    is_active           BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
59	    failed_login_count  INT DEFAULT 0,
60	    locked_until        TIMESTAMPTZ,
61	    last_login_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
62	    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
63	    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
64	);
65	
66	CREATE TABLE user_roles (
67	    user_id    INT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
68	    role_id    INT NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
69	    granted_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
70	    granted_by INT REFERENCES users(id),
71	    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, role_id)
72	);
73	
74	CREATE TABLE sessions (
75	    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
76	    user_id         INT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
77	    token_hash      TEXT NOT NULL,
78	    status          session_status DEFAULT 'active',
79	    ip_address      INET,
80	    user_agent      TEXT,
81	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
82	    last_active_at  TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
83	    expires_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
84	    idle_timeout_s  INT DEFAULT 900,        -- 15 minutes
85	    max_lifetime_s  INT DEFAULT 28800       -- 8 hours
86	);
87	
88	CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_user ON sessions(user_id);
89	CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_token ON sessions(token_hash);
90	CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_status ON sessions(status) WHERE status = 'active';
91	
92	-- ============================================================
93	-- CONFIGURATION CENTER
94	-- ============================================================
95	
96	CREATE TABLE feature_flags (
97	    id                  SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
98	    key                 VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,

101	    rollout_strategy    flag_rollout_strategy DEFAULT 'disabled',
102	    rollout_percentage  INT DEFAULT 0 CHECK (rollout_percentage BETWEEN 0 AND 100),

108

120

144	
145	-- ============================================================

326	-- ============================================================
327	-- Synonym Conflict Detection Trigger
328	-- Blocks two active synonyms from pointing to different canonical tags
329	-- ============================================================
330	
331	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION check_synonym_conflict()
332	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
333	DECLARE
334	    existing_canonical INT;
335	BEGIN
336	    IF NEW.status = 'active' THEN
337	        SELECT canonical_tag_id INTO existing_canonical
338	        FROM taxonomy_synonyms
339	        WHERE term = NEW.term
340	          AND status = 'active'
341	          AND canonical_tag_id != NEW.canonical_tag_id
342	          AND id != COALESCE(NEW.id, 0)
343	        LIMIT 1;
344	
345	        IF existing_canonical IS NOT NULL THEN
346	            RAISE EXCEPTION
347	                'Synonym conflict: term "%" already has an active mapping to canonical tag ID %. '
348	                'Deactivate the existing synonym first or use the same canonical tag.',
349	                NEW.term, existing_canonical;
350	        END IF;
351	    END IF;
352	    RETURN NEW;
353	END;
354	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
355	
356	CREATE TRIGGER trg_synonym_conflict_check
357	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON taxonomy_synonyms
358	    FOR EACH ROW
359	    EXECUTE FUNCTION check_synonym_conflict();

412	-- ============================================================
413	-- Auto-update search_vector on resource insert/update
414	-- Combines title (weight A), description (weight B), content_body (weight C),
415	-- and pinyin_title (weight D) into tsvector
416	-- ============================================================
417	
418	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION resources_search_vector_update()
419	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
420	BEGIN
421	    NEW.search_vector :=
422	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.title, '')), 'A') ||
423	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.description, '')), 'B') ||
424	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.content_body, '')), 'C') ||
425	        setweight(to_tsvector('simple', COALESCE(NEW.pinyin_title, '')), 'D');
426	    RETURN NEW;
427	END;
428	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
429	
430	CREATE TRIGGER trg_resources_search_vector
431	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OF title, description, content_body, pinyin_title
432	    ON resources
433	    FOR EACH ROW
434	    EXECUTE FUNCTION resources_search_vector_update();

436	-- ============================================================
437	-- Archive pages view (auto-generated by month and tag)
438	-- ============================================================
439	
440	CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS resource_archive_monthly AS
441	SELECT
442	    date_trunc('month', published_at) AS archive_month,
443	    category_id,
444	    COUNT(*) AS resource_count,
445	    array_agg(id ORDER BY published_at DESC) AS resource_ids
446	FROM resources
447	WHERE status = 'published' AND published_at IS NOT NULL
448	GROUP BY date_trunc('month', published_at), category_id
449	ORDER BY archive_month DESC;
450	
451	CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS resource_archive_by_tag AS
452	SELECT
453	    rt.tag_id,
454	    t.name AS tag_name,
455	    date_trunc('month', r.published_at) AS archive_month,
456	    COUNT(*) AS resource_count,
457	    array_agg(r.id ORDER BY r.published_at DESC) AS resource_ids
458	FROM resources r
459	JOIN resource_tags rt ON rt.resource_id = r.id
460	JOIN taxonomy_tags t ON t.id = rt.tag_id
461	WHERE r.status = 'published' AND r.published_at IS NOT NULL
462	GROUP BY rt.tag_id, t.name, date_trunc('month', r.published_at)
463	ORDER BY archive_month DESC;

776	    evidence_urls       TEXT[],
777	    evidence_metadata_enc BYTEA,
778	    merchant_response   TEXT,
779	    arbitration_notes   TEXT,
780	    arbitration_outcome VARCHAR(30),
781	    arbitrated_by       INT REFERENCES users(id),
782	    arbitrated_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
783	    created_by          INT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
784	    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
785	    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
786	);
787	
788	CREATE INDEX idx_disputes_review ON disputes(review_id);
789	CREATE INDEX idx_disputes_vendor ON disputes(vendor_id);
790	CREATE INDEX idx_disputes_status ON disputes(status);
791	
792	-- Dispute State Machine Trigger
793	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION validate_dispute_transition()
794	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
795	BEGIN
796	    IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.status != NEW.status THEN
797	        IF NOT (
798	            (OLD.status = 'created'           AND NEW.status = 'evidence_uploaded') OR
799	            (OLD.status = 'evidence_uploaded'  AND NEW.status = 'under_review')     OR
800	            (OLD.status = 'under_review'       AND NEW.status = 'arbitration')      OR
801	            (OLD.status = 'arbitration'        AND NEW.status IN ('resolved_hidden', 'resolved_disclaimer', 'resolved_restored', 'rejected'))
802	        ) THEN
803	            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid dispute transition from % to %', OLD.status, NEW.status;
804	        END IF;
805	    END IF;
806	    RETURN NEW;
807	END;
808	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
809	
810	CREATE TRIGGER trg_dispute_state_machine
811	    BEFORE UPDATE ON disputes
812	    FOR EACH ROW
813	    EXECUTE FUNCTION validate_dispute_transition();
814	
815	-- Invoice Variance Auto-Classify Trigger
816	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION auto_classify_invoice_variance()
817	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
818	DECLARE
819	    threshold NUMERIC(14,2);
820	BEGIN
821	    IF NEW.variance_amount IS NOT NULL AND NEW.status = 'variance_detected' THEN
822	        SELECT CAST(value AS NUMERIC) INTO threshold
823	        FROM configs WHERE key = 'variance.auto_writeoff_threshold';
824	        IF threshold IS NULL THEN threshold := 5.00; END IF;
825	        IF ABS(NEW.variance_amount) < threshold THEN
826	            NEW.status := 'pending_approval';
827	        ELSE
828	            NEW.status := 'manual_investigation';
829	        END IF;
830	    END IF;
831	    RETURN NEW;
832	END;
833	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
834	
835	CREATE TRIGGER trg_invoice_variance_classify
836	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OF variance_amount, status ON invoices
837	    FOR EACH ROW
838	    EXECUTE FUNCTION auto_classify_invoice_variance();
839	
840	-- No seeded data: all content is created by users at runtime.

190	func (r *ProcurementRepository) MatchInvoice(ctx context.Context, invoiceID, orderID, matchedBy int) error {
191		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, `
192			UPDATE invoices SET
193				order_id = $2,
194				order_amount = (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2),
195				variance_amount = invoice_amount - (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2),
196				variance_pct = CASE
197					WHEN (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2) = 0 THEN 0
198					ELSE ROUND(((invoice_amount - (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2))
199						/ (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2)) * 100, 2)
200				END,
201				status = CASE
202					WHEN invoice_amount = (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2)
203					THEN 'matched'
204					ELSE 'variance_detected'
205				END,
206				matched_by = $3,
207				matched_at = NOW(),
208				updated_at = NOW()
209			WHERE id = $1
210		`, invoiceID, orderID, matchedBy)
211		return err
212	}

283	// CompareStatement totals all AR entries for a vendor in a period and compares against a statement.
284	func (r *ProcurementRepository) SumLedgerByVendor(ctx context.Context, vendorID int, entryType, periodStart, periodEnd string) (float64, error) {
285		query := `
286			SELECT COALESCE(SUM(amount), 0)
287			FROM ledger_entries
288			WHERE vendor_id = $1 AND entry_type = $2
289		`
290		args := []interface{}{vendorID, entryType}
291		idx := 3
292		if periodStart != "" {
293			query += fmt.Sprintf(" AND posted_at >= $%d", idx)
294			args = append(args, periodStart)
295			idx++
296		}
297		if periodEnd != "" {
298			query += fmt.Sprintf(" AND posted_at <= $%d::date + INTERVAL '1 day'", idx)
299			args = append(args, periodEnd)
300			idx++
301		}
302	
303		var total float64
304		err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, query, args...).Scan(&total)
305		return total, err
306	}

**Schema highlights (842 lines):**

  - User Progress Tracking
  - Recommendations (pre-computed by background worker)

- **Procurement Module:**
  - Vendors, Orders, Invoices
  - Ledger Entries (AR/AP)
  - Settlements with variance detection
  - Billing Rules
  - Vendor Reviews with dispute workflow
  
- **Database Triggers:**
  - Synonym conflict detection
  - Invoice variance auto-classification
  - Dispute state machine validation
  - Resource search vector update
  
- **Materialized Views:**
  - Resource archive by month and tag

---

### 5. KEY FILES - FULL CONTENT

#### **A. `/backend/internal/handlers/auth.go` (240 lines)**

Implements authentication endpoints:
- **POST /api/auth/login** - Username/password login with session creation
- **POST /api/auth/mfa/verify** - MFA code verification
- **POST /api/auth/mfa/setup** - Setup MFA (returns QR code)
- **POST /api/auth/mfa/confirm** - Confirm MFA enablement
- **POST /api/auth/mfa/disable** - Disable MFA
- **POST /api/auth/logout** - Session invalidation
- **GET /api/auth/me** - Current user claims
- **POST /api/auth/register** (Public) - User registration with role self-selection
- **GET /api/admin/users** (Admin) - List all users
- **POST /api/admin/users/assign-role** (Admin) - Assign roles to users

Key features:
- Role-based registration (learner, procurement_specialist, approver, finance_analyst, content_moderator, system_admin)
- Password validation (min 8 chars)
- MFA support
- Admin role assignment

---

#### **B. `/backend/internal/models/register.go` (41 lines)**

Data models for registration:
- **RegisterRequest:** username, email, password, display_name, role, job_family, department, cost_center
- **AssignRoleRequest:** user_id, role
- **RegisterResponse:** message, user (with roles)
- **ValidRoles():** Returns self-selectable roles (excludes system_admin)
- **AllRoles():** Returns all roles including system_admin

---

#### **C. `/backend/internal/services/recommendation_worker.go` (398 lines)**

Background worker for ML-based recommendations:
- Runs nightly rebuild loop computing user-item similarity matrices
- **Diversity control:** Max 40% of recommendations from single category (configurable)
- **Cold-start strategy:** Job-family matching + global popularity for new users
- **Collaborative filtering:** Cosine similarity on interaction vectors
- **Functions:**
  - `Start(interval)` - Launches nightly rebuild
  - `computeForUser()` - Generates recommendations for single user
  - `coldStartDefaults()` - Job-family & popularity-based defaults
  - `collaborativeFilter()` - User-user similarity recommendations
  - `applyDiversityControl()` - Category quota enforcement
  - `cosineSimilarity()` - Sparse vector similarity

---

#### **D. `/backend/internal/middleware/auth_middleware.go` (178 lines)**

JWT and authorization middleware:
- **RequireAuth():** Validates JWT, checks session (idle/max lifetime), injects claims
- **RequireRole():** Role-based access (returns 404 for invisibility)
- **RequirePermission():** Permission-based access (returns 404 for invisibility)
- **AppVersionCheck():** Client version validation with read-only grace period
- Stores in context: claims, user_id, username, roles, permissions, session_id

---

#### **E. `/backend/internal/repository/procurement_repo.go` (690 lines)**

Comprehensive procurement data access layer:

**Orders:**
- `GetOrders()`, `GetOrderByID()` - Retrieve with vendor info
- `GetOrderLineItems()` - Line item queries
- `UpdateOrderStatus()` - Status transitions with approval tracking

**Invoices:**
- `GetInvoices()`, `GetInvoiceByID()` - Retrieval
- `MatchInvoice()` - Match to order, compute variance, auto-classify
- `UpdateInvoiceStatus()` - Status changes

**Ledger (AR/AP):**
- `GetLedgerEntries()` - Query AR/AP entries by vendor/period
- `CreateLedgerEntry()` - Insert ledger entries
- `SumLedgerByVendor()` - Sum AR/AP by period

**Settlements:**
- `GetSettlements()`, `GetSettlementByID()` - Retrieve settlements
- `UpdateSettlement()` - Transition with approval/write-off tracking

**Cost Allocation:**
- `GetCostAllocation()` - Report by department/cost center

**Billing Rules:**
- `GetBillingRules()` - Query rules by type

**Vendors:**
- `GetVendors()` - Active vendor list

**Reviews & Disputes:**
- `GetReviews()`, `CreateReview()` - Vendor reviews
- `CreateMerchantReply()` - Merchant response
- `GetDisputes()`, `GetDisputeByID()` - Dispute queries
- `CreateDispute()` - Initiate dispute
- `UpdateReviewStatus()` - Apply dispute arbitration outcome
- `TransitionDispute()` - State machine transitions

---

#### **F. `/backend/internal/services/reconciliation.go` (299 lines)**

Finance & procurement reconciliation state machines:

**CompareStatement():**
- Compares vendor statement total vs. ledger AR entries
- Computes variance & variance %
- Auto-suggest write-off if < threshold ($5 default)
- Returns suggested state (matched/pending_approval/manual_investigation)

**MatchInvoice():**
- Match invoice to order
- Validates order status (fulfilled/approved)
- Delegates to DB trigger for variance computation & auto-classification

**TransitionSettlement():**
- Valid state transitions:
  - open → matched → variance_pending → writeoff_suggested → writeoff_approved → settled
  - any open state → disputed
  - matched → settled (no variance)

**TransitionDispute():**
- Valid state transitions:
  - created → evidence_uploaded → under_review → arbitration → (resolved_hidden | resolved_disclaimer | resolved_restored | rejected)
- Supports merchant responses during review phases
- Applies arbitration outcome to review status

**Utility Functions:**
- `applyArbitrationOutcome()` - Updates review visibility based on dispute resolution
- `GetLedgerForExport()`, `GetSettlementsForExport()` - CSV export preparation

---

#### **G. `/backend/internal/handlers/procurement.go` (497 lines)**

Procurement API endpoints:

**Vendors:**
- `GET /api/procurement/vendors` - List active vendors

**Orders:**
- `GET /api/procurement/orders[?status=...]` - List orders
- `GET /api/procurement/orders/:id` - Order detail with line items
- `PUT /api/procurement/orders/:id/approve` - Approve order

**Invoices:**
- `GET /api/procurement/invoices[?status=...]` - List invoices
- `GET /api/procurement/invoices/:id` - Invoice detail
- `POST /api/procurement/invoices/match` - Match invoice to order

**Reconciliation:**
- `POST /api/procurement/reconciliation/compare` - Compare statement vs. ledger
- `GET /api/procurement/ledger[?vendor_id=...&type=...]` - Ledger entries
- `GET /api/procurement/cost-allocation` - Cost report
- `GET /api/procurement/billing-rules[?type=...]` - Billing rules

**Settlements:**
- `GET /api/procurement/settlements[?status=...]` - List settlements
- `POST /api/procurement/settlements/transition` - State transitions
- `POST /api/procurement/settlements` - Create settlement
- `POST /api/procurement/ledger` - Create ledger entry

**Reviews:**
- `POST /api/procurement/reviews` - Create review
- `GET /api/procurement/reviews[?vendor_id=...]` - List reviews
- `POST /api/procurement/reviews/reply` - Merchant reply

**Disputes:**
- `POST /api/procurement/disputes` - Create dispute
- `GET /api/procurement/disputes[?status=...]` - List disputes
- `GET /api/procurement/disputes/:id` - Dispute detail
- `POST /api/procurement/disputes/transition` - Dispute state transitions

**Exports:**
- `GET /api/procurement/export/ledger[?vendor_id=...]` - CSV export
- `GET /api/procurement/export/settlements` - CSV export

---

## TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE SUMMARY

**Backend Stack:** Go + Echo framework + PostgreSQL 15+ with pgcrypto extensions

**Key Patterns:**
- Service-Repository-Handler layered architecture
- RBAC with granular permissions
- State machines for settlements & disputes (DB triggers + service validation)
- Background worker for ML recommendations (nightly batch)
- Session management with idle timeout & max lifetime
- AES-256 encryption for sensitive data (MFA secrets, recovery codes)
- Full-text search with pinyin support for Chinese content
- Materialized views for archive aggregations

**Frontend Stack:** React.jsx + Vite, state management with Zustand or custom stores

**Database:** PostgreSQL with 20+ tables, comprehensive indexing (GIN trigrams, TSVECTOR), audit logging, and sophisticated triggers for data validation

192			UPDATE invoices SET
193				order_id = $2,
194				order_amount = (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2),
195				variance_amount = invoice_amount - (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2),
196				variance_pct = CASE
197					WHEN (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2) = 0 THEN 0
198					ELSE ROUND(((invoice_amount - (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2))
199						/ (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2)) * 100, 2)
200				END,
201				status = CASE
202					WHEN invoice_amount = (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2)
203					THEN 'matched'
204					ELSE 'variance_detected'
205				END,
206				matched_by = $3,
207				matched_at = NOW(),
208				updated_at = NOW()
209			WHERE id = $1
210		`, invoiceID, orderID, matchedBy)

653	// TransitionDispute updates the dispute status. The DB trigger trg_dispute_state_machine
654	// enforces valid transitions and will raise an exception for invalid ones.
655	func (r *ProcurementRepository) TransitionDispute(ctx context.Context, id int, newStatus string, updates map[string]interface{}) error {
656		query := "UPDATE disputes SET status = $2, updated_at = NOW()"
657		args := []interface{}{id, newStatus}
658		idx := 3
659	
660		if v, ok := updates["evidence_urls"]; ok {
661			query += fmt.Sprintf(", evidence_urls = $%d", idx)
662			args = append(args, v)
663			idx++
664		}
665		if v, ok := updates["merchant_response"]; ok {
666			query += fmt.Sprintf(", merchant_response = $%d", idx)
667			args = append(args, v)
668			idx++
669		}
670		if v, ok := updates["arbitration_notes"]; ok {
671			query += fmt.Sprintf(", arbitration_notes = $%d", idx)
672			args = append(args, v)
673			idx++
674		}
675		if v, ok := updates["arbitration_outcome"]; ok {
676			query += fmt.Sprintf(", arbitration_outcome = $%d", idx)
677			args = append(args, v)
678			idx++
679		}
680		if v, ok := updates["arbitrated_by"]; ok {
681			query += fmt.Sprintf(", arbitrated_by = $%d, arbitrated_at = NOW()", idx)
682			args = append(args, v)
683			idx++
684		}
685	
686		query += " WHERE id = $1"
687		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, query, args...)
688		return err
689	}

68		MatchedBy       *int       `json:"matched_by,omitempty"`
69		MatchedAt       *time.Time `json:"matched_at,omitempty"`
70		CreatedAt       time.Time  `json:"created_at"`
71		UpdatedAt       time.Time  `json:"updated_at"`
72	}
73	
74	// ---- Ledger ----
75	
76	type LedgerEntry struct {
77		ID            int       `json:"id"`
78		EntryType     string    `json:"entry_type"` // AR, AP
79		ReferenceType string    `json:"reference_type"`
80		ReferenceID   int       `json:"reference_id"`
81		VendorID      int       `json:"vendor_id"`
82		VendorName    string    `json:"vendor_name,omitempty"`
83		Amount        float64   `json:"amount"`
84		Currency      string    `json:"currency"`
85		Department    *string   `json:"department,omitempty"`
86		CostCenter    *string   `json:"cost_center,omitempty"`
87		Description   *string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
88		PostedBy      *int      `json:"posted_by,omitempty"`
89		PostedAt      time.Time `json:"posted_at"`
90		CreatedAt     time.Time `json:"created_at"`
91	}
92	
93	// ---- Settlements ----
94	
95	type Settlement struct {
96		ID              int        `json:"id"`
97		VendorID        int        `json:"vendor_id"`
98		VendorName      string     `json:"vendor_name,omitempty"`
99		Status          string     `json:"status"`
100		ARTotal         float64    `json:"ar_total"`
101		APTotal         float64    `json:"ap_total"`
102		NetAmount       float64    `json:"net_amount"`
103		VarianceAmount  float64    `json:"variance_amount"`
104		WriteoffAmount  float64    `json:"writeoff_amount"`
105		Department      *string    `json:"department,omitempty"`
106		CostCenter      *string    `json:"cost_center,omitempty"`
107		PeriodStart     *string    `json:"period_start,omitempty"`
108		PeriodEnd       *string    `json:"period_end,omitempty"`
109		Notes           *string    `json:"notes,omitempty"`
110		CreatedBy       *int       `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
111		ApprovedBy      *int       `json:"approved_by,omitempty"`
112		ApprovedAt      *time.Time `json:"approved_at,omitempty"`
113		SettledAt       *time.Time `json:"settled_at,omitempty"`
114		CreatedAt       time.Time  `json:"created_at"`
115		UpdatedAt       time.Time  `json:"updated_at"`
116	}
117	
118	type BillingRule struct {
119		ID            int     `json:"id"`
120		RuleType      string  `json:"rule_type"`
121		Code          string  `json:"code"`
122		Description   *string `json:"description,omitempty"`
123		RatePerUnit   float64 `json:"rate_per_unit"`
124		MinCharge     float64 `json:"min_charge"`
125		MaxCharge     *float64 `json:"max_charge,omitempty"`
126		EffectiveFrom string  `json:"effective_from"`
127		EffectiveTo   *string `json:"effective_to,omitempty"`
128		IsActive      bool    `json:"is_active"`
129	}
130	
131	// ---- Reviews & Disputes ----
132	
133	type VendorReview struct {
134		ID             int              `json:"id"`
135		VendorID       int              `json:"vendor_id"`
136		VendorName     string           `json:"vendor_name,omitempty"`
137		OrderID        *int             `json:"order_id,omitempty"`
138		ReviewerID     int              `json:"reviewer_id"`
139		Rating         int              `json:"rating"`
140		Title          *string          `json:"title,omitempty"`
141		Body           string           `json:"body"`
142		ImageURLs      []string         `json:"image_urls,omitempty"`
143		ReviewStatus   string           `json:"review_status"`
144		DisclaimerText *string          `json:"disclaimer_text,omitempty"`
145		Replies        []MerchantReply  `json:"replies,omitempty"`
146		CreatedAt      time.Time        `json:"created_at"`
147		UpdatedAt      time.Time        `json:"updated_at"`
148	}
149	
150	type MerchantReply struct {
151		ID        int       `json:"id"`
152		ReviewID  int       `json:"review_id"`
153		VendorID  int       `json:"vendor_id"`
154		Body      string    `json:"body"`
155		RepliedBy *int      `json:"replied_by,omitempty"`
156		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
157	}
158	
159	type Dispute struct {
160		ID                  int        `json:"id"`
161		ReviewID            int        `json:"review_id"`
162		VendorID            int        `json:"vendor_id"`
163		VendorName          string     `json:"vendor_name,omitempty"`
164		Status              string     `json:"status"`
165		Reason              string     `json:"reason"`
166		EvidenceURLs        []string   `json:"evidence_urls,omitempty"`
167		MerchantResponse    *string    `json:"merchant_response,omitempty"`
168		ArbitrationNotes    *string    `json:"arbitration_notes,omitempty"`
169		ArbitrationOutcome  *string    `json:"arbitration_outcome,omitempty"`
170		ArbitratedBy        *int       `json:"arbitrated_by,omitempty"`
171		ArbitratedAt        *time.Time `json:"arbitrated_at,omitempty"`
172		CreatedBy           int        `json:"created_by"`
173		CreatedAt           time.Time  `json:"created_at"`
174		UpdatedAt           time.Time  `json:"updated_at"`
175	}
176	
177	// ---- Requests ----
178	
179	type MatchInvoiceRequest struct {
180		InvoiceID int `json:"invoice_id" validate:"required"`
181		OrderID   int `json:"order_id" validate:"required"`
182	}
183	
184	type CompareStatementRequest struct {
185		VendorID    int     `json:"vendor_id" validate:"required"`
186		StatementTotal float64 `json:"statement_total" validate:"required"`
187		PeriodStart string  `json:"period_start"`
188		PeriodEnd   string  `json:"period_end"`
189	}
190	
191	type CompareStatementResponse struct {
192		VendorID       int     `json:"vendor_id"`
193		StatementTotal float64 `json:"statement_total"`
194		LedgerTotal    float64 `json:"ledger_total"`
195		Variance       float64 `json:"variance"`
196		VariancePct    float64 `json:"variance_pct"`
197		AutoWriteoff   bool    `json:"auto_writeoff"`
198		SuggestedState string  `json:"suggested_state"`
199	}
200	
201	type SettlementTransitionRequest struct {
202		SettlementID int    `json:"settlement_id" validate:"required"`
203		Action       string `json:"action" validate:"required"` // approve_writeoff, settle, dispute
204		Notes        string `json:"notes,omitempty"`
205	}
206	
207	type DisputeTransitionRequest struct {
208		DisputeID         int    `json:"dispute_id" validate:"required"`
209		Action            string `json:"action" validate:"required"`
210		EvidenceURLs      []string `json:"evidence_urls,omitempty"`
211		MerchantResponse  string `json:"merchant_response,omitempty"`
212		ArbitrationNotes  string `json:"arbitration_notes,omitempty"`
213		ArbitrationOutcome string `json:"arbitration_outcome,omitempty"` // hide, disclaimer, restore
214	}
215	
216	type CostAllocationReport struct {
217		Department  string  `json:"department"`
218		CostCenter  string  `json:"cost_center"`
219		ARTotal     float64 `json:"ar_total"`
220		APTotal     float64 `json:"ap_total"`
221		NetAmount   float64 `json:"net_amount"`
222		EntryCount  int     `json:"entry_count"`
223	}
224	
225	// ---- Create Requests ----
226	
227	type CreateReviewRequest struct {
228		VendorID  int      `json:"vendor_id" validate:"required"`
229		OrderID   *int     `json:"order_id,omitempty"`
230		Rating    int      `json:"rating" validate:"required"`
231		Title     *string  `json:"title,omitempty"`
232		Body      string   `json:"body" validate:"required"`
233		ImageURLs []string `json:"image_urls,omitempty"`
234	}
235	
236	type CreateMerchantReplyRequest struct {
237		ReviewID int    `json:"review_id" validate:"required"`
238		Body     string `json:"body" validate:"required"`
239	}
240	
241	type CreateDisputeRequest struct {
242		ReviewID int    `json:"review_id" validate:"required"`
243		VendorID int    `json:"vendor_id" validate:"required"`
244		Reason   string `json:"reason" validate:"required"`
245	}
246	
247	type CreateLedgerEntryRequest struct {
248		EntryType     string  `json:"entry_type" validate:"required"` // AR or AP
249		ReferenceType string  `json:"reference_type" validate:"required"`
250		ReferenceID   int     `json:"reference_id" validate:"required"`
251		VendorID      int     `json:"vendor_id" validate:"required"`
252		Amount        float64 `json:"amount" validate:"required"`
253		Currency      string  `json:"currency,omitempty"`
254		Department    *string `json:"department,omitempty"`
255		CostCenter    *string `json:"cost_center,omitempty"`
256		Description   *string `json:"description,omitempty"`
257	}
258	
259	type CreateSettlementRequest struct {
260		VendorID    int     `json:"vendor_id" validate:"required"`
261		ARTotal     float64 `json:"ar_total"`
262		APTotal     float64 `json:"ap_total"`
263		Department  *string `json:"department,omitempty"`
264		CostCenter  *string `json:"cost_center,omitempty"`
265		PeriodStart *string `json:"period_start,omitempty"`
266		PeriodEnd   *string `json:"period_end,omitempty"`
267		Notes       *string `json:"notes,omitempty"`
268	}

1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"log"
7		"os"
8		"os/signal"
9		"syscall"
10		"time"
11	
12		"wlpr-portal/internal/handlers"
13		"wlpr-portal/internal/middleware"
14		"wlpr-portal/internal/repository"
15		"wlpr-portal/internal/services"
16		"wlpr-portal/pkg/crypto"
17		"wlpr-portal/pkg/jwt"
18	
19		"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
20		"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
21		echomw "github.com/labstack/echo/v4/middleware"
22	)
23	
24	func main() {
25		// Initialize encryption key
26		if err := crypto.InitEncryptionKey(); err != nil {
27			log.Fatalf("Failed to init encryption key: %v", err)
28		}
29	
30		// Initialize JWT secret
31		if err := jwt.InitJWTSecret(); err != nil {
32			log.Fatalf("Failed to init JWT secret: %v", err)
33		}
34	
35		// Database connection
36		dbURL := os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL")
37		if dbURL == "" {
38			dbURL = "postgres://wlpr:wlpr_secret@localhost:5432/wlpr_portal?sslmode=disable"
39		}
40	
41		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
42		defer cancel()
43	
44		dbPool, err := pgxpool.New(ctx, dbURL)
45		if err != nil {
46			log.Fatalf("Failed to connect to database: %v", err)
47		}
48		defer dbPool.Close()
49	
50		if err := dbPool.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
51			log.Fatalf("Failed to ping database: %v", err)
52		}
53		log.Println("Connected to PostgreSQL")
54	
55		// Repositories
56		userRepo := repository.NewUserRepository(dbPool)
57		configRepo := repository.NewConfigRepository(dbPool)
58		taxonomyRepo := repository.NewTaxonomyRepository(dbPool)
59		searchRepo := repository.NewSearchRepository(dbPool)
60		learningRepo := repository.NewLearningRepository(dbPool)
61	
62		// Services
63		configService := services.NewConfigService(configRepo)
64		if err := configService.LoadAll(ctx); err != nil {
65			log.Fatalf("Failed to load initial configs: %v", err)
66		}
67		configService.StartBackgroundSync(ctx, 30*time.Second)
68	
69		authService := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, configRepo)
70		mfaService := services.NewMFAService(userRepo)
71		searchService := services.NewSearchService(searchRepo, taxonomyRepo, configService)
72		learningService := services.NewLearningService(learningRepo, searchRepo)
73		procRepo := repository.NewProcurementRepository(dbPool)
74		reconService := services.NewReconciliationService(procRepo, configService)
75	
76		// Start recommendation worker (nightly rebuild, runs every 24h)
77		recWorker := services.NewRecommendationWorker(learningRepo, searchRepo, configService)
78		recWorker.Start(ctx, 24*time.Hour)
79	
80		// Handlers
81		authHandler := handlers.NewAuthHandler(authService, mfaService, userRepo)
82		configHandler := handlers.NewConfigHandler(configService)
83		searchHandler := handlers.NewSearchHandler(searchService)
84		taxonomyHandler := handlers.NewTaxonomyHandler(taxonomyRepo)
85		learningHandler := handlers.NewLearningHandler(learningService)
86		procHandler := handlers.NewProcurementHandler(procRepo, reconService)
87	
88		// Middleware
89		authMW := middleware.NewAuthMiddleware(authService, configService)
90	
91		// Echo setup
92		e := echo.New()
93		e.HideBanner = true
94	
95		// Global middleware
96		e.Use(echomw.Recover())
97		e.Use(middleware.RequestLogger())
98		e.Use(echomw.CORSWithConfig(echomw.CORSConfig{
99			AllowOrigins:     []string{"http://localhost:5173", "http://localhost:3000"},
100			AllowMethods:     []string{"GET", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE", "OPTIONS"},
101			AllowHeaders:     []string{"Authorization", "Content-Type", "X-App-Version"},
102			ExposeHeaders:    []string{"X-App-Deprecated", "X-Min-Version"},
103			AllowCredentials: true,
104		}))
105		e.Use(authMW.AppVersionCheck())
106	
107		// Health check
108		e.GET("/api/health", func(c echo.Context) error {
109			return c.JSON(200, map[string]string{"status": "ok"})
110		})
111	
112		// Public auth routes (no JWT required)
113		auth := e.Group("/api/auth")
114		auth.POST("/login", authHandler.Login)
115		auth.POST("/register", authHandler.Register)
116		auth.POST("/mfa/verify", authHandler.VerifyMFA)
117	
118		// Protected auth routes
119		authProtected := e.Group("/api/auth", authMW.RequireAuth())
120		authProtected.POST("/logout", authHandler.Logout)
121		authProtected.GET("/me", authHandler.Me)
122		authProtected.POST("/mfa/setup", authHandler.SetupMFA)
123		authProtected.POST("/mfa/confirm", authHandler.ConfirmMFA)
124		authProtected.POST("/mfa/disable", authHandler.DisableMFA)
125	
126		// Admin user management (system_admin only)
127		adminGroup := e.Group("/api/admin", authMW.RequireAuth(),
128			authMW.RequireRole("system_admin"))
129		adminGroup.GET("/users", authHandler.ListUsers)
130		adminGroup.POST("/users/assign-role", authHandler.AssignRole)
131	
132		// Config routes (admin only)
133		configGroup := e.Group("/api/config",
134			authMW.RequireAuth(),
135			authMW.RequireRole("system_admin"),
136		)
137		configGroup.GET("/all", configHandler.GetAllConfigs)
138		configGroup.GET("/:key", configHandler.GetConfig)
139		configGroup.PUT("/:key", configHandler.UpdateConfig)
140		configGroup.GET("/flags", configHandler.GetAllFlags)
141		configGroup.GET("/flags/:key", configHandler.GetFlag)
142		configGroup.PUT("/flags/:key", configHandler.UpdateFlag)
143		configGroup.GET("/flags/:key/check", configHandler.CheckFlag)
144	
145		// Public config check (for feature flag evaluation by authenticated users)
146		flagCheck := e.Group("/api/flags", authMW.RequireAuth())
147		flagCheck.GET("/:key/check", configHandler.CheckFlag)
148	
149		// ================================================================
150		// MODULE 2: Catalog & Taxonomy
151		// Learner: Read (search/browse). ContentMod: CRUD. Admin: CRUD.
152		// Finance/Procurement: No access.
153		// ================================================================
154		catalogRead := e.Group("/api", authMW.RequireAuth(),
155			authMW.RequireRole("learner", "content_moderator", "system_admin"))
156		catalogRead.GET("/search", searchHandler.Search)
157		catalogRead.GET("/resources/:id", searchHandler.GetResource)
158		catalogRead.GET("/archives", searchHandler.GetArchives)
159	
160		taxRead := e.Group("/api/taxonomy", authMW.RequireAuth(),
161			authMW.RequireRole("learner", "content_moderator", "system_admin"))
162		taxRead.GET("/tags", taxonomyHandler.GetTags)
163		taxRead.GET("/tags/hierarchy", taxonomyHandler.GetHierarchy)
164		taxRead.GET("/tags/:id", taxonomyHandler.GetTag)
165		taxRead.GET("/synonyms/:tag_id", taxonomyHandler.GetSynonyms)
166	
167		taxWrite := e.Group("/api/taxonomy", authMW.RequireAuth(),
168			authMW.RequireRole("content_moderator", "system_admin"))
169		taxWrite.POST("/tags", taxonomyHandler.CreateTag)
170		taxWrite.POST("/synonyms", taxonomyHandler.CreateSynonym)
171		taxWrite.GET("/review-queue", taxonomyHandler.GetReviewQueue)
172	
173		// ================================================================
174		// MODULE 1: Learning & Development
175		// Learner: Read (assigned/public), Update (own progress/enroll).
176		// ContentMod: CRUD (resources & paths). Admin: CRUD (all).
177		// Finance/Procurement: No access.
178		// ================================================================
179		learnRead := e.Group("/api/learning", authMW.RequireAuth(),
180			authMW.RequireRole("learner", "content_moderator", "system_admin"))
181		learnRead.GET("/paths", learningHandler.GetPaths)
182		learnRead.GET("/paths/:id", learningHandler.GetPath)
183		learnRead.GET("/recommendations", learningHandler.GetRecommendations)
184	
185		// Learner can enroll/update own progress/export own records
186		learnSelf := e.Group("/api/learning", authMW.RequireAuth(),
187			authMW.RequireRole("learner", "content_moderator", "system_admin"))
188		learnSelf.POST("/enroll", learningHandler.Enroll)
189		learnSelf.DELETE("/enroll/:path_id", learningHandler.DropEnrollment)
190		learnSelf.GET("/enrollments", learningHandler.GetEnrollments)
191		learnSelf.GET("/enrollments/:path_id", learningHandler.GetEnrollmentDetail)
192		learnSelf.PUT("/progress", learningHandler.UpdateProgress)
193		learnSelf.GET("/progress", learningHandler.GetProgress)
194		learnSelf.GET("/export", learningHandler.ExportCSV)
195	
196		// ================================================================
197		// MODULE 3: Procurement & Disputes
198		// ProcSpec: Create/Read/Update orders+reviews. Approver: Read+Approve.
199		// ContentMod: Read appeals + arbitrate (hide/show/disclaimer).
200		// Finance: Read settlement view. Admin: Full.
201		// Learner: No access.
202		// ================================================================
203	
204		// Procurement read (orders, invoices, vendors, billing rules)
205		procRead := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
206			authMW.RequireRole("procurement_specialist", "approver", "finance_analyst", "system_admin"))
207		procRead.GET("/vendors", procHandler.GetVendors)
208		procRead.GET("/orders", procHandler.GetOrders)
209		procRead.GET("/orders/:id", procHandler.GetOrder)
210		procRead.GET("/invoices", procHandler.GetInvoices)
211		procRead.GET("/invoices/:id", procHandler.GetInvoice)
212		procRead.GET("/billing-rules", procHandler.GetBillingRules)
213	
214		// Procurement write: create reviews with ratings/text/images, merchant replies
215		procWrite := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
216			authMW.RequireRole("procurement_specialist", "system_admin"))
217		procWrite.GET("/reviews", procHandler.GetReviews)
218		procWrite.POST("/reviews", procHandler.CreateReview)
219		procWrite.POST("/reviews/reply", procHandler.CreateMerchantReply)
220	
221		// Approval actions (approve orders, match invoices)
222		procApprove := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
223			authMW.RequireRole("approver", "system_admin"))
224		procApprove.PUT("/orders/:id/approve", procHandler.ApproveOrder)
225		procApprove.POST("/invoices/match", procHandler.MatchInvoice)
226	
227		// Dispute read (procurement_specialist, content_moderator, approver, admin)
228		disputeRead := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
229			authMW.RequireRole("procurement_specialist", "content_moderator", "approver", "system_admin"))
230		disputeRead.GET("/disputes", procHandler.GetDisputes)
231		disputeRead.GET("/disputes/:id", procHandler.GetDispute)
232	
233		// Dispute creation (procurement users create appeals with evidence)
234		disputeCreate := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
235			authMW.RequireRole("procurement_specialist", "system_admin"))
236		disputeCreate.POST("/disputes", procHandler.CreateDispute)
237	
238		// Dispute arbitration transitions (content_moderator + admin)
239		disputeArbitrate := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
240			authMW.RequireRole("content_moderator", "system_admin"))
241		disputeArbitrate.POST("/disputes/transition", procHandler.TransitionDispute)
242	
243		// ================================================================
244		// MODULE 4: Reconciliation & Finance
245		// Finance: Create AR/AP, Read all, Update settlement status.
246		// Approver: Read variances, Approve write-offs.
247		// ProcSpec: Read relevant order costs only.
248		// Learner/ContentMod: No access.
249		// ================================================================
250	
251		// Finance: read ledger, create AR/AP entries, create settlements, compare, export
252		finRead := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
253			authMW.RequireRole("finance_analyst", "system_admin"))
254		finRead.GET("/ledger", procHandler.GetLedger)
255		finRead.POST("/ledger", procHandler.CreateLedgerEntry)
256		finRead.GET("/cost-allocation", procHandler.GetCostAllocation)
257		finRead.POST("/reconciliation/compare", procHandler.CompareStatement)
258		finRead.POST("/settlements", procHandler.CreateSettlement)
259		finRead.GET("/export/ledger", procHandler.ExportLedger)
260		finRead.GET("/export/settlements", procHandler.ExportSettlements)
261	
262		// Settlements: finance reads + manages, approver reads + approves
263		settleGroup := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
264			authMW.RequireRole("finance_analyst", "approver", "system_admin"))
265		settleGroup.GET("/settlements", procHandler.GetSettlements)
266		settleGroup.POST("/settlements/transition", procHandler.TransitionSettlement)
267	
268		// Start server
269		port := os.Getenv("PORT")
270		if port == "" {
271			port = "8080"
272		}
273	
274		go func() {
275			addr := fmt.Sprintf(":%s", port)
276			log.Printf("Starting server on %s", addr)
277			if err := e.Start(addr); err != nil {
278				log.Printf("Server stopped: %v", err)
279			}
280		}()
281	
282		// Graceful shutdown
283		quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
284		signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
285		<-quit
286	
287		log.Println("Shutting down server...")
288		shutdownCtx, shutdownCancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
289		defer shutdownCancel()
290		if err := e.Shutdown(shutdownCtx); err != nil {
291			log.Printf("Server forced shutdown: %v", err)
292		}
293		cancel()
294		log.Println("Server exited")
295	}

1	package repository
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"time"
6	
7		"wlpr-portal/internal/models"
8	
9		"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"
10		"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
11	)
12	
13	type UserRepository struct {
14		db *pgxpool.Pool
15	}
16	
17	func NewUserRepository(db *pgxpool.Pool) *UserRepository {
18		return &UserRepository{db: db}
19	}
20	
21	func (r *UserRepository) FindByUsername(ctx context.Context, username string) (*models.User, error) {
22		user := &models.User{}
23		err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, `
24			SELECT id, username, email, password_hash, display_name,
25				   mfa_enabled, mfa_secret_enc, mfa_recovery_enc,
26				   job_family, department, cost_center,
27				   is_active, failed_login_count, locked_until,
28				   last_login_at, created_at, updated_at
29			FROM users WHERE username = $1
30		`, username).Scan(
31			&user.ID, &user.Username, &user.Email, &user.PasswordHash, &user.DisplayName,
32			&user.MFAEnabled, &user.MFASecretEnc, &user.MFARecoveryEnc,
33			&user.JobFamily, &user.Department, &user.CostCenter,

67			SELECT r.id, r.name, COALESCE(r.description, '')
68			FROM roles r
69			JOIN user_roles ur ON ur.role_id = r.id
70			WHERE ur.user_id = $1

76

331	
332	// IsSessionValid checks idle timeout and max lifetime.
333	func IsSessionValid(session *models.Session) bool {
334		now := time.Now()
335		if session.Status != "active" {
336			return false
337		}
338		if now.After(session.ExpiresAt) {
339			return false
340		}
341		idleDeadline := session.LastActiveAt.Add(time.Duration(session.IdleTimeoutS) * time.Second)
342		if now.After(idleDeadline) {
343			return false
344		}
345		maxDeadline := session.CreatedAt.Add(time.Duration(session.MaxLifetimeS) * time.Second)
346		if now.After(maxDeadline) {
347			return false
348		}
349		return true
350	}

326	-- ============================================================
327	-- Synonym Conflict Detection Trigger
328	-- Blocks two active synonyms from pointing to different canonical tags
329	-- ============================================================
330	
331	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION check_synonym_conflict()
332	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
333	DECLARE
334	    existing_canonical INT;
335	BEGIN
336	    IF NEW.status = 'active' THEN
337	        SELECT canonical_tag_id INTO existing_canonical
338	        FROM taxonomy_synonyms
339	        WHERE term = NEW.term
340	          AND status = 'active'
341	          AND canonical_tag_id != NEW.canonical_tag_id
342	          AND id != COALESCE(NEW.id, 0)
343	        LIMIT 1;
344	
345	        IF existing_canonical IS NOT NULL THEN
346	            RAISE EXCEPTION
347	                'Synonym conflict: term "%" already has an active mapping to canonical tag ID %. '
348	                'Deactivate the existing synonym first or use the same canonical tag.',
349	                NEW.term, existing_canonical;
350	        END IF;
351	    END IF;
352	    RETURN NEW;
353	END;
354	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
355	
356	CREATE TRIGGER trg_synonym_conflict_check
357	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON taxonomy_synonyms
358	    FOR EACH ROW
359	    EXECUTE FUNCTION check_synonym_conflict();

412	-- ============================================================
413	-- Auto-update search_vector on resource insert/update
414	-- Combines title (weight A), description (weight B), content_body (weight C),
415	-- and pinyin_title (weight D) into tsvector
416	-- ============================================================
417	
418	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION resources_search_vector_update()
419	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
420	BEGIN
421	    NEW.search_vector :=
422	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.title, '')), 'A') ||
423	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.description, '')), 'B') ||
424	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.content_body, '')), 'C') ||
425	        setweight(to_tsvector('simple', COALESCE(NEW.pinyin_title, '')), 'D');
426	    RETURN NEW;
427	END;
428	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
429	
430	CREATE TRIGGER trg_resources_search_vector
431	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OF title, description, content_body, pinyin_title
432	    ON resources
433	    FOR EACH ROW
434	    EXECUTE FUNCTION resources_search_vector_update();

436	-- ============================================================
437	-- Archive pages view (auto-generated by month and tag)
438	-- ============================================================
439	
440	CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS resource_archive_monthly AS
441	SELECT
442	    date_trunc('month', published_at) AS archive_month,
443	    category_id,
444	    COUNT(*) AS resource_count,
445	    array_agg(id ORDER BY published_at DESC) AS resource_ids
446	FROM resources
447	WHERE status = 'published' AND published_at IS NOT NULL
448	GROUP BY date_trunc('month', published_at), category_id
449	ORDER BY archive_month DESC;
450	
451	CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS resource_archive_by_tag AS
452	SELECT
453	    rt.tag_id,
454	    t.name AS tag_name,
455	    date_trunc('month', r.published_at) AS archive_month,
456	    COUNT(*) AS resource_count,
457	    array_agg(r.id ORDER BY r.published_at DESC) AS resource_ids
458	FROM resources r
459	JOIN resource_tags rt ON rt.resource_id = r.id
460	JOIN taxonomy_tags t ON t.id = rt.tag_id
461	WHERE r.status = 'published' AND r.published_at IS NOT NULL
462	GROUP BY rt.tag_id, t.name, date_trunc('month', r.published_at)
463	ORDER BY archive_month DESC;

782	    arbitrated_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
783	    created_by          INT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
784	    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
785	    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
786	);
787	
788	CREATE INDEX idx_disputes_review ON disputes(review_id);
789	CREATE INDEX idx_disputes_vendor ON disputes(vendor_id);
790	CREATE INDEX idx_disputes_status ON disputes(status);
791	
792	-- Dispute State Machine Trigger
793	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION validate_dispute_transition()
794	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
795	BEGIN
796	    IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.status != NEW.status THEN
797	        IF NOT (
798	            (OLD.status = 'created'           AND NEW.status = 'evidence_uploaded') OR
799	            (OLD.status = 'evidence_uploaded'  AND NEW.status = 'under_review')     OR
800	            (OLD.status = 'under_review'       AND NEW.status = 'arbitration')      OR
801	            (OLD.status = 'arbitration'        AND NEW.status IN ('resolved_hidden', 'resolved_disclaimer', 'resolved_restored', 'rejected'))
802	        ) THEN
803	            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid dispute transition from % to %', OLD.status, NEW.status;
804	        END IF;
805	    END IF;
806	    RETURN NEW;
807	END;
808	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
809	
810	CREATE TRIGGER trg_dispute_state_machine
811	    BEFORE UPDATE ON disputes
812	    FOR EACH ROW
813	    EXECUTE FUNCTION validate_dispute_transition();
814	
815	-- Invoice Variance Auto-Classify Trigger
816	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION auto_classify_invoice_variance()
817	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
818	DECLARE
819	    threshold NUMERIC(14,2);
820	BEGIN
821	    IF NEW.variance_amount IS NOT NULL AND NEW.status = 'variance_detected' THEN
822	        SELECT CAST(value AS NUMERIC) INTO threshold
823	        FROM configs WHERE key = 'variance.auto_writeoff_threshold';
824	        IF threshold IS NULL THEN threshold := 5.00; END IF;
825	        IF ABS(NEW.variance_amount) < threshold THEN
826	            NEW.status := 'pending_approval';
827	        ELSE
828	            NEW.status := 'manual_investigation';
829	        END IF;
830	    END IF;
831	    RETURN NEW;
832	END;
833	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
834	
835	CREATE TRIGGER trg_invoice_variance_classify
836	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OF variance_amount, status ON invoices
837	    FOR EACH ROW
838	    EXECUTE FUNCTION auto_classify_invoice_variance();
839	
840	-- No seeded data: all content is created by users at runtime.
841

16	func init() {
17		// Initialize JWT secret for test token generation.

25	// generateTestToken creates a signed JWT with the specified user attributes.
26	func generateTestToken(t *testing.T, userID int, username string, roles, permissions []string) string {
27		t.Helper()
28		token, err := jwt.GenerateToken(userID, username, "test-session-id", roles, permissions)

30			t.Fatalf("failed to generate test token: %v", err)

34	
35	// jwtMiddleware is a simplified auth middleware for tests.
36	// It validates the JWT and injects claims into the echo context,
37	// mirroring the real RequireAuth middleware behavior.
38	func jwtMiddleware(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
39		return func(c echo.Context) error {
40			authHeader := c.Request().Header.Get("Authorization")
41			if authHeader == "" {
42				return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusUnauthorized, "missing authorization header")
43			}
44			parts := strings.SplitN(authHeader, " ", 2)
45			if len(parts) != 2 || strings.ToLower(parts[0]) != "bearer" {
46				return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid authorization format")
47			}
48			claims, err := jwt.ValidateToken(parts[1])
49			if err != nil {
50				return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid token")
51			}
52			c.Set("claims", claims)
53			c.Set("user_id", claims.UserID)
54			c.Set("username", claims.Username)
55			c.Set("roles", claims.Roles)
56			c.Set("permissions", claims.Permissions)
57			c.Set("session_id", claims.SessionID)
58			return next(c)
59		}
60	}
61	
62	// requireRole is a simplified role-check middleware for tests.
63	func requireRole(roles ...string) echo.MiddlewareFunc {
64		return func(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
65			return func(c echo.Context) error {
66				userRoles, ok := c.Get("roles").([]string)
67				if !ok || len(userRoles) == 0 {
68					return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusNotFound)
69				}
70				for _, required := range roles {
71					for _, userRole := range userRoles {
72						if required == userRole {
73							return next(c)
74						}
75					}
76				}
77				return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusNotFound)
78			}
79		}
80	}

// MaskedReview is a DTO that hides reviewer identity for non-admin views.
type MaskedReview struct {
	ID             int              `json:"id"`
	VendorID       int              `json:"vendor_id"`
	VendorName     string           `json:"vendor_name,omitempty"`
	OrderID        *int             `json:"order_id,omitempty"`
	Rating         int              `json:"rating"`
	Title          *string          `json:"title,omitempty"`
	Body           string           `json:"body"`
	ImageURLs      []string         `json:"image_urls,omitempty"`
	ReviewStatus   string           `json:"review_status"`
	DisclaimerText *string          `json:"disclaimer_text,omitempty"`
	Replies        []MerchantReply  `json:"replies,omitempty"`
	CreatedAt      time.Time        `json:"created_at"`
	UpdatedAt      time.Time        `json:"updated_at"`
}

// MaskReview strips reviewer_id and other PII from a review for non-admin views.
func MaskReview(r VendorReview) MaskedReview {
	return MaskedReview{
		ID:             r.ID,
		VendorID:       r.VendorID,
		VendorName:     r.VendorName,
		OrderID:        r.OrderID,
		Rating:         r.Rating,
		Title:          r.Title,
		Body:           r.Body,
		ImageURLs:      r.ImageURLs,
		ReviewStatus:   r.ReviewStatus,
		DisclaimerText: r.DisclaimerText,
		Replies:        r.Replies,
		CreatedAt:      r.CreatedAt,
		UpdatedAt:      r.UpdatedAt,
	}
}

// MaskReviews applies MaskReview to a slice of reviews.
func MaskReviews(reviews []VendorReview) []MaskedReview {
	masked := make([]MaskedReview, len(reviews))
	for i, r := range reviews {
		masked[i] = MaskReview(r)
	}
	return masked
}"}

13	type ProcurementRepository struct {

15	}
16	
17	func NewProcurementRepository(db *pgxpool.Pool) *ProcurementRepository {
18		return &ProcurementRepository{db: db}
19	}
20	
21	// ---- Orders ----
22	
23	func (r *ProcurementRepository) GetOrders(ctx context.Context, status string) ([]models.ProcurementOrder, error) {
24		query := `
25			SELECT o.id, o.order_number, o.vendor_id, v.name, o.status,
26				   o.department, o.cost_center, o.total_amount, o.currency,
27				   o.description, o.submitted_by, o.approved_by, o.approved_at,
28				   o.created_at, o.updated_at
29			FROM procurement_orders o
30			JOIN vendors v ON v.id = o.vendor_id
31		`
32		args := []interface{}{}
33		if status != "" {
34			query += " WHERE o.status = $1"
35			args = append(args, status)
36		}
37		query += " ORDER BY o.created_at DESC"
38	
39		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, query, args...)
40		if err != nil {
41			return nil, err
42		}
43		defer rows.Close()
44	
45		orders := make([]models.ProcurementOrder, 0)
46		for rows.Next() {
47			var o models.ProcurementOrder
48			if err := rows.Scan(
49				&o.ID, &o.OrderNumber, &o.VendorID, &o.VendorName, &o.Status,
50				&o.Department, &o.CostCenter, &o.TotalAmount, &o.Currency,
51				&o.Description, &o.SubmittedBy, &o.ApprovedBy, &o.ApprovedAt,
52				&o.CreatedAt, &o.UpdatedAt,
53			); err != nil {
54				return nil, err
55			}
56			orders = append(orders, o)
57		}
58		return orders, rows.Err()
59	}
60	
61	func (r *ProcurementRepository) GetOrderByID(ctx context.Context, id int) (*models.ProcurementOrder, error) {
62		o := &models.ProcurementOrder{}
63		err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, `
64			SELECT o.id, o.order_number, o.vendor_id, v.name, o.status,
65				   o.department, o.cost_center, o.total_amount, o.currency,
66				   o.description, o.submitted_by, o.approved_by, o.approved_at,
67				   o.created_at, o.updated_at
68			FROM procurement_orders o
69			JOIN vendors v ON v.id = o.vendor_id
70			WHERE o.id = $1
71		`, id).Scan(
72			&o.ID, &o.OrderNumber, &o.VendorID, &o.VendorName, &o.Status,
73			&o.Department, &o.CostCenter, &o.TotalAmount, &o.Currency,
74			&o.Description, &o.SubmittedBy, &o.ApprovedBy, &o.ApprovedAt,
75			&o.CreatedAt, &o.UpdatedAt,
76		)
77		if err == pgx.ErrNoRows {
78			return nil, nil
79		}
80		if err != nil {
81			return nil, err
82		}
83	
84		items, err := r.GetOrderLineItems(ctx, id)
85		if err == nil {
86			o.LineItems = items
87		}
88		return o, nil
89	}

107				return nil, err

111		return items, rows.Err()

115		if approvedBy != nil {
116			_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, `
117				UPDATE procurement_orders SET status = $2, approved_by = $3, approved_at = NOW(), updated_at = NOW()

169		inv := &models.Invoice{}
170		err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, `
171			SELECT i.id, i.invoice_number, i.order_id, i.vendor_id, v.name,
172				   i.status, i.invoice_amount, i.order_amount, i.variance_amount,
173				   i.variance_pct, i.currency, i.invoice_date::text, i.due_date::text,
174				   i.notes, i.matched_by, i.matched_at, i.created_at, i.updated_at
175			FROM invoices i
176			JOIN vendors v ON v.id = i.vendor_id
177			WHERE i.id = $1
178		`, id).Scan(
179			&inv.ID, &inv.InvoiceNumber, &inv.OrderID, &inv.VendorID, &inv.VendorName,
180			&inv.Status, &inv.InvoiceAmount, &inv.OrderAmount, &inv.VarianceAmount,
181			&inv.VariancePct, &inv.Currency, &inv.InvoiceDate, &inv.DueDate,
182			&inv.Notes, &inv.MatchedBy, &inv.MatchedAt, &inv.CreatedAt, &inv.UpdatedAt,
183		)

185			return nil, nil
186		}
187		return inv, err
188	}

190	func (r *ProcurementRepository) MatchInvoice(ctx context.Context, invoiceID, orderID, matchedBy int) error {
191		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, `
192			UPDATE invoices SET
193				order_id = $2,
194				order_amount = (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2),
195				variance_amount = invoice_amount - (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2),
196				variance_pct = CASE
197					WHEN (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2) = 0 THEN 0
198					ELSE ROUND(((invoice_amount - (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2))
199						/ (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2)) * 100, 2)
200				END,
201				status = CASE
202					WHEN invoice_amount = (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2)
203					THEN 'matched'
204					ELSE 'variance_detected'
205				END,
206				matched_by = $3,
207				matched_at = NOW(),
208				updated_at = NOW()
209			WHERE id = $1
210		`, invoiceID, orderID, matchedBy)
211		return err
212	}

223	func (r *ProcurementRepository) GetLedgerEntries(ctx context.Context, vendorID int, entryType string) ([]models.LedgerEntry, error) {
224		query := `
225			SELECT le.id, le.entry_type, le.reference_type, le.reference_id,
226				   le.vendor_id, v.name, le.amount, le.currency,
227				   le.department, le.cost_center, le.description,
228				   le.posted_by, le.posted_at, le.created_at
229			FROM ledger_entries le
230			JOIN vendors v ON v.id = le.vendor_id
231			WHERE 1=1
232		`
233		args := []interface{}{}
234		idx := 1
235	
236		if vendorID > 0 {
237			query += fmt.Sprintf(" AND le.vendor_id = $%d", idx)
238			args = append(args, vendorID)
239			idx++
240		}
241		if entryType != "" {
242			query += fmt.Sprintf(" AND le.entry_type = $%d", idx)
243			args = append(args, entryType)
244			idx++
245		}
246		query += " ORDER BY le.posted_at DESC"
247	
248		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, query, args...)
249		if err != nil {
250			return nil, err
251		}
252		defer rows.Close()
253	
254		entries := make([]models.LedgerEntry, 0)
255		for rows.Next() {
256			var le models.LedgerEntry
257			if err := rows.Scan(
258				&le.ID, &le.EntryType, &le.ReferenceType, &le.ReferenceID,
259				&le.VendorID, &le.VendorName, &le.Amount, &le.Currency,
260				&le.Department, &le.CostCenter, &le.Description,
261				&le.PostedBy, &le.PostedAt, &le.CreatedAt,
262			); err != nil {
263				return nil, err
264			}
265			entries = append(entries, le)
266		}
267		return entries, rows.Err()
268	}

283	// CompareStatement totals all AR entries for a vendor in a period and compares against a statement.
284	func (r *ProcurementRepository) SumLedgerByVendor(ctx context.Context, vendorID int, entryType, periodStart, periodEnd string) (float64, error) {
285		query := `
286			SELECT COALESCE(SUM(amount), 0)
287			FROM ledger_entries
288			WHERE vendor_id = $1 AND entry_type = $2
289		`
290		args := []interface{}{vendorID, entryType}
291		idx := 3
292		if periodStart != "" {
293			query += fmt.Sprintf(" AND posted_at >= $%d", idx)
294			args = append(args, periodStart)
295			idx++
296		}
297		if periodEnd != "" {
298			query += fmt.Sprintf(" AND posted_at <= $%d::date + INTERVAL '1 day'", idx)
299			args = append(args, periodEnd)
300			idx++
301		}
302	
303		var total float64
304		err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, query, args...).Scan(&total)
305		return total, err
306	}

380			query += ", approved_by = $4, approved_at = NOW()"
381			args = append(args, *approvedBy)
382		}

384			query += ", settled_at = NOW()"
385		}
386		if status == "writeoff_approved" {
387			query += ", writeoff_amount = variance_amount"
388		}
389		query += " WHERE id = $1"
390		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, query, args...)
391		return err
392	}

396	func (r *ProcurementRepository) GetCostAllocation(ctx context.Context) ([]models.CostAllocationReport, error) {
397		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, `
398			SELECT COALESCE(department, 'Unassigned'), COALESCE(cost_center, 'N/A'),
399				   COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN entry_type = 'AR' THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0) AS ar_total,
400				   COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN entry_type = 'AP' THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0) AS ap_total,
401				   COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN entry_type = 'AR' THEN amount ELSE -amount END), 0) AS net,
402				   COUNT(*) AS entry_count
403			FROM ledger_entries
404			GROUP BY department, cost_center
405			ORDER BY department, cost_center
406		`)
407		if err != nil {
408			return nil, err
409		}
410		defer rows.Close()
411	
412		reports := make([]models.CostAllocationReport, 0)
413		for rows.Next() {
414			var r models.CostAllocationReport
415			if err := rows.Scan(&r.Department, &r.CostCenter, &r.ARTotal, &r.APTotal, &r.NetAmount, &r.EntryCount); err != nil {
416				return nil, err
417			}
418			reports = append(reports, r)
419		}
420		return reports, rows.Err()
421	}

653	// TransitionDispute updates the dispute status. The DB trigger trg_dispute_state_machine
654	// enforces valid transitions and will raise an exception for invalid ones.
655	func (r *ProcurementRepository) TransitionDispute(ctx context.Context, id int, newStatus string, updates map[string]interface{}) error {
656		query := "UPDATE disputes SET status = $2, updated_at = NOW()"
657		args := []interface{}{id, newStatus}
658		idx := 3
659	
660		if v, ok := updates["evidence_urls"]; ok {
661			query += fmt.Sprintf(", evidence_urls = $%d", idx)
662			args = append(args, v)
663			idx++
664		}
665		if v, ok := updates["evidence_metadata_enc"]; ok {
666			query += fmt.Sprintf(", evidence_metadata_enc = $%d", idx)
667			args = append(args, v)
668			idx++
669		}
670		if v, ok := updates["merchant_response"]; ok {
671			query += fmt.Sprintf(", merchant_response = $%d", idx)
672			args = append(args, v)
673			idx++
674		}
675		if v, ok := updates["arbitration_notes"]; ok {
676			query += fmt.Sprintf(", arbitration_notes = $%d", idx)
677			args = append(args, v)
678			idx++
679		}
680		if v, ok := updates["arbitration_outcome"]; ok {
681			query += fmt.Sprintf(", arbitration_outcome = $%d", idx)
682			args = append(args, v)
683			idx++
684		}
685		if v, ok := updates["arbitrated_by"]; ok {
686			query += fmt.Sprintf(", arbitrated_by = $%d, arbitrated_at = NOW()", idx)
687			args = append(args, v)
688			idx++
689		}
690	
691		query += " WHERE id = $1"
692		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, query, args...)
693		return err
694	}
695

1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"log"
7		"os"
8		"os/signal"
9		"syscall"
10		"time"
11	
12		"wlpr-portal/internal/handlers"
13		"wlpr-portal/internal/middleware"
14		"wlpr-portal/internal/repository"
15		"wlpr-portal/internal/services"
16		"wlpr-portal/pkg/crypto"
17		"wlpr-portal/pkg/jwt"
18	
19		"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
20		"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
21		echomw "github.com/labstack/echo/v4/middleware"
22	)
23	
24	func main() {
25		// Initialize encryption key
26		if err := crypto.InitEncryptionKey(); err != nil {
27			log.Fatalf("Failed to init encryption key: %v", err)
28		}
29	
30		// Initialize JWT secret
31		if err := jwt.InitJWTSecret(); err != nil {
32			log.Fatalf("Failed to init JWT secret: %v", err)
33		}
34	
35		// Database connection
36		dbURL := os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL")
37		if dbURL == "" {
38			dbURL = "postgres://wlpr:wlpr_secret@localhost:5432/wlpr_portal?sslmode=disable"
39		}
40	
41		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
42		defer cancel()
43	
44		dbPool, err := pgxpool.New(ctx, dbURL)
45		if err != nil {
46			log.Fatalf("Failed to connect to database: %v", err)
47		}
48		defer dbPool.Close()
49	
50		if err := dbPool.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
51			log.Fatalf("Failed to ping database: %v", err)
52		}
53		log.Println("Connected to PostgreSQL")
54	
55		// Repositories
56		userRepo := repository.NewUserRepository(dbPool)
57		configRepo := repository.NewConfigRepository(dbPool)
58		taxonomyRepo := repository.NewTaxonomyRepository(dbPool)
59		searchRepo := repository.NewSearchRepository(dbPool)
60		learningRepo := repository.NewLearningRepository(dbPool)
61	
62		// Services
63		configService := services.NewConfigService(configRepo)
64		if err := configService.LoadAll(ctx); err != nil {
65			log.Fatalf("Failed to load initial configs: %v", err)
66		}
67		configService.StartBackgroundSync(ctx, 30*time.Second)
68	
69		authService := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, configRepo)
70		mfaService := services.NewMFAService(userRepo)
71		searchService := services.NewSearchService(searchRepo, taxonomyRepo, configService)
72		learningService := services.NewLearningService(learningRepo, searchRepo)
73		procRepo := repository.NewProcurementRepository(dbPool)
74		reconService := services.NewReconciliationService(procRepo, configService)
75		exportSinkService := services.NewExportSinkService(configService)
76	
77		// Start recommendation worker (nightly rebuild, runs every 24h)
78		recWorker := services.NewRecommendationWorker(learningRepo, searchRepo, configService)
79		recWorker.Start(ctx, 24*time.Hour)
80	
81		// Handlers
82		authHandler := handlers.NewAuthHandler(authService, mfaService, userRepo)
83		configHandler := handlers.NewConfigHandler(configService)
84		searchHandler := handlers.NewSearchHandler(searchService)
85		taxonomyHandler := handlers.NewTaxonomyHandler(taxonomyRepo)
86		learningHandler := handlers.NewLearningHandler(learningService)
87		procHandler := handlers.NewProcurementHandler(procRepo, reconService, exportSinkService)
88	
89		// Middleware
90		authMW := middleware.NewAuthMiddleware(authService, configService)
91	
92		// Echo setup
93		e := echo.New()
94		e.HideBanner = true
95	
96		// Global middleware
97		e.Use(echomw.Recover())
98		e.Use(middleware.RequestLogger())
99		e.Use(echomw.CORSWithConfig(echomw.CORSConfig{
100			AllowOrigins:     []string{"http://localhost:5173", "http://localhost:3000"},
101			AllowMethods:     []string{"GET", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE", "OPTIONS"},
102			AllowHeaders:     []string{"Authorization", "Content-Type", "X-App-Version"},
103			ExposeHeaders:    []string{"X-App-Deprecated", "X-Min-Version"},
104			AllowCredentials: true,
105		}))
106		e.Use(authMW.AppVersionCheck())
107	
108		// Health check
109		e.GET("/api/health", func(c echo.Context) error {
110			return c.JSON(200, map[string]string{"status": "ok"})
111		})
112	
113		// Public auth routes (no JWT required)
114		auth := e.Group("/api/auth")
115		auth.POST("/login", authHandler.Login)
116		auth.POST("/register", authHandler.Register)
117		auth.POST("/mfa/verify", authHandler.VerifyMFA)
118	
119		// Protected auth routes
120		authProtected := e.Group("/api/auth", authMW.RequireAuth())

### Fix 1: Block self-assigned privileged roles at registration (Blocker)

**Files modified:**
- `backend/internal/handlers/auth.go` - Registration now uses `CountUsers()` for first-user bootstrap (auto-assigns `system_admin`). Subsequent users validate against `ValidRoles()` which excludes `system_admin`.
- `frontend/src/pages/Register.jsx` - Removed `system_admin` from the ROLE_OPTIONS dropdown.
- `backend/internal/handlers/auth_handler_test.go` - Updated tests: `TestRegister_SystemAdminRoleBlocked` verifies `system_admin` is not in `ValidRoles()`, `TestRegister_SystemAdminSelfAssign_Rejected` confirms the security boundary.
- `frontend/src/test/Register.test.jsx` - Updated to assert `system_admin` is NOT in the dropdown.

### Fix 2: Implement encrypted dispute evidence metadata (High)

**Files modified:**
- `backend/internal/models/procurement.go` - Added `EvidenceMetadataEnc` (encrypted bytes, never serialized to JSON) and `EvidenceMetadata` (decrypted, JSON-visible for admins only) fields to `Dispute`.
- `backend/internal/repository/procurement_repo.go` - Updated `GetDisputes`, `GetDisputeByID` to read `evidence_metadata_enc` from DB. Updated `TransitionDispute` to write `evidence_metadata_enc`.
- `backend/internal/services/reconciliation.go` - Added `crypto` import and AES encryption of evidence metadata during `upload_evidence` action.
- `backend/internal/handlers/procurement.go` - Added `decryptDisputeMetadata()` helper. Dispute endpoints decrypt metadata only for admin/moderator users.

### Fix 3: Add offline finance export sinks - file-drop and LAN webhook (High)

**Files created:**
- `backend/internal/services/export_sink.go` - New `ExportSinkService` with file-drop writer, LAN webhook sender, retry logic (configurable retries with delay), and compensation (fallback to file-drop on webhook failure).

**Files modified:**
- `backend/internal/handlers/procurement.go` - Updated `ProcurementHandler` to include `exportSink`, updated `NewProcurementHandler` signature, `ExportLedger`/`ExportSettlements` now also dispatch to sinks in background goroutines.
- `backend/cmd/api/main.go` - Wired `ExportSinkService` into the handler.
- `backend/init.sql` - Added 4 config entries: `export.file_drop_dir`, `export.webhook_url`, `export.max_retries`, `export.retry_delay_seconds`.

### Fix 4: Fix recommendation diversity enforcement (High)

**Files modified:**
- `backend/internal/services/recommendation_worker.go` - `applyDiversityControl()` now re-checks category quota during the deferred refill phase. Items that exceed the cap are skipped entirely rather than blindly appended.
- `backend/internal/services/learning_test.go` - Added 4 deterministic tests: `TestDiversityControl_EnforcesMaxCategoryPct`, `TestDiversityControl_DeferredItemsAlsoChecked`, `TestDiversityControl_EmptyInput`, `TestDiversityControl_SingleCategory`.

### Fix 5: Implement 14-day read-only grace logic for version compatibility (Medium)

**Files modified:**
- `backend/internal/middleware/auth_middleware.go` - `AppVersionCheck()` now reads `app.read_only_grace_days` config and `GetConfigUpdatedAt()` to determine when min_version was set. Within grace: read-only (GET/HEAD/OPTIONS allowed, writes blocked with days-remaining message). After grace: all requests hard-blocked.
- `backend/internal/services/config_service.go` - Added `GetConfigUpdatedAt()` method returning the `updated_at` timestamp for a config key.

### Fix 6: Personal-data masking in non-admin views (Medium)

**Files modified:**
- `backend/internal/models/procurement.go` - Added `MaskedReview` DTO (strips `ReviewerID`), `MaskReview()` and `MaskReviews()` helper functions.
- `backend/internal/handlers/procurement.go` - `GetReviews` now returns `MaskedReview` DTOs for non-admin/non-moderator users, hiding `reviewer_id`.

196	// MaskReview strips reviewer_id and other PII from a review for non-admin views.
197	func MaskReview(r VendorReview) MaskedReview {
198		return MaskedReview{
199			ID:             r.ID,
200			VendorID:       r.VendorID,
201			VendorName:     r.VendorName,
202			OrderID:        r.OrderID,
203			Rating:         r.Rating,
204			Title:          r.Title,
205			Body:           r.Body,
206			ImageURLs:      r.ImageURLs,
207			ReviewStatus:   r.ReviewStatus,
208			DisclaimerText: r.DisclaimerText,
209			Replies:        r.Replies,
210			CreatedAt:      r.CreatedAt,
211			UpdatedAt:      r.UpdatedAt,
212		}
213	}
214	
215	// MaskReviews applies MaskReview to a slice of reviews.
216	func MaskReviews(reviews []VendorReview) []MaskedReview {
217		masked := make([]MaskedReview, len(reviews))
218		for i, r := range reviews {
219			masked[i] = MaskReview(r)
220		}
221		return masked
222	}

content.text:
The agent's concern about test helpers is incorrect - Go test files in the same package share all functions. Files in `backend/internal/handlers/` are all `package handlers`, so `generateTestToken`, `jwtMiddleware`, `requireRole` from `api_security_test.go` are available to all handler test files. Similarly, `compareVersions` from `auth_middleware.go` is accessible to `version_middleware_test.go` since both are `package middleware`.

Found 0 total occurrences across 0 files.

Good, `models` is used 12 times. All code is clean. Here's a summary of all changes made in this round:

---

### Fix 1: Complete dispute payload masking for non-admin roles

**`backend/internal/models/procurement.go`** - Added `MaskedDispute` DTO struct that omits `CreatedBy`, `ArbitratedBy`, and `EvidenceMetadata` fields. Added `MaskDispute()` and `MaskDisputes()` helper functions.

**`backend/internal/handlers/procurement.go`** - Updated `GetDisputes` and `GetDispute` handlers: admin/moderator users get full dispute data with decrypted evidence metadata; non-admin users get `MaskedDispute` DTOs with user-linked fields stripped.

### Fix 2: Backend tests for export sink runtime behavior

**`backend/internal/services/export_sink_test.go`** (new) - 11 tests covering:
- File-drop: writes CSV file with correct content for ledger and settlements
- No sinks configured: returns nil
- Webhook success: single call on HTTP 200
- Webhook retries: retries on failure, succeeds on 3rd attempt
- Webhook fallback: compensates with file-drop when all retries fail
- Webhook all-fail: returns error
- CSV generation: validates header and data rows
- Config: defaults and custom values

### Fix 3: Middleware tests for version grace-period boundaries

**`backend/internal/middleware/version_middleware_test.go`** (new) - 15 tests covering:
- `compareVersions`: equal, less-than, greater-than
- No version header: passes through
- Current version: POST allowed
- Old version in grace: GET allowed, POST/PUT/DELETE blocked
- Grace expired: GET blocked, POST blocked
- Grace boundary: day 13 of 14 (in grace), day 15 of 14 (expired)
- Custom grace days: 7-day grace within/expired

### Fix 4: Registration privilege boundary tests + dispute masking tests

**`backend/internal/handlers/auth_handler_test.go`** - Added `TestRegister_PrivilegeBoundary_FirstUserVsSubsequent` with 5 assertions: system_admin not in ValidRoles, system_admin in AllRoles, all 5 non-privileged roles present, ValidRoles is subset of AllRoles, exactly 1 role (system_admin) excluded.

**`backend/internal/handlers/procurement_handler_test.go`** - Added:
- `TestGetReviews_NonAdminGetsMaskedResponse`: procurement_specialist doesn't see `reviewer_id`, admin does
- `TestGetDisputes_NonAdminGetsMaskedResponse`: procurement_specialist doesn't see `created_by`/`arbitrated_by`, admin and content_moderator do

**`backend/internal/services/config_service.go`** - Added `NewConfigServiceForTest()` for creating test-only config services without DB.
